sections.paragraph:
Ἀθῆναι, ῶν, αἱ (Hom. et al.; Philo, Joseph.) Athens, capital of Attica Ac 17:15f; 18:1; 1 Th 3:1 (also in subscr. to 1 and 2 Th and Hb). Cp. Haenchen on Ac 17:15 (lit.); OBroneer, BA 21, ’58, 2–28.—DDD s.v. Athena.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

subscr. subscr. = subscription

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

BA BA = Biblical Archaeologist—List 6

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 24
